rationale: The citing paper uses Lanz et al. (2019) as key support for a core premise of its framework: users who are still unknown are more receptive to endorsement requests. That claim is invoked in the methodology rationale, cost/acceptance assumptions, and discussion of why a forward-looking seeding strategy is viable, so the citation informs conceptual setup rather than serving as a one-off mention. Although the 2024 paper does not directly reuse the 2019 model, it relies on it to justify the strategic logic behind targeting prospective influencers.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Lanz et al. (2019) as prior empirical grounding that unknown users are more receptive to endorsement requests, which underpins the forward-looking strategy of contracting prospective influencers early. The new study then builds a different simulation-based decision framework on top of that premise.
